content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base scaffold that can be enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent LLM self-reflection perspectives, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement pipeline that retains Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while improving reflection accuracy. This hybrid approach solves a hidden limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: that single-perspective self-feedback often overlooks blind spots in the LLM’s initial output, just as a person cannot spot their own logical gaps relying only on their own first way of thinking.
